name: firebase_get_environment
description: Use this to retrieve the current Firebase environment configuration for the Firebase CLI and Firebase MCP server, including current authenticated user, project directory, active Firebase Project, and more.

name: firebase_update_environment
description: Use this to update environment config for the Firebase CLI and Firebase MCP server, such as project directory, active project, active user account, accept terms of service, and more.

project_dir.description: Change the current project directory - this should be a directory that has a firebase.json file (or will have one).

active_project.description: Change the active project for the current project directory. Should be a Firebase project ID or configured project alias.

active_user_account.description: The email address of the signed-in user to authenticate as when interacting with the current project directory.

name: firebase_login
description: Use this to sign the user into the Firebase CLI and Firebase MCP server. This requires a Google Account.

authCode.description: The authorization code from the login flow

name: firebase_logout
description: Use this to sign the user out of the Firebase CLI and Firebase MCP server.

email.description: The email of the account to log out. If not provided, all accounts will be logged out.

name: firebase_list_projects
description: Use this to retrieve a list of Firebase Projects that the signed-in user has access to.

page_size.description: the number of projects to list per page (defaults to 1000)

page_token.description: the page token to start listing from

name: firebase_get_project
description: Use this to retrieve information about the currently active Firebase Project.

name: firebase_create_project
description: Use this to create a new Firebase Project.

project_id.description: The project ID to create or use.

display_name.description: The user-friendly display name for the project.

name: firebase_list_apps
description: Use this to retrieve a list of the Firebase Apps registered in the currently active Firebase project. Firebase Apps can be iOS, Android, or Web.

platform.description: the specific platform to list (omit to list all platforms)

name: firebase_create_app
description: Use this to create a new Firebase App in the currently active Firebase Project. Firebase Apps can be iOS, Android, or Web.

display_name.description: The user-friendly display name for your app.

platform.description: The platform for which to create an app.

android_config.description: Configuration for Android apps. Required if platform is 'android'.

package_name.description: The package name for your Android app (e.g., com.example.myapp).

ios_config.description: Configuration for iOS apps. Required if platform is 'ios'.

bundle_id.description: The bundle ID for your iOS app (e.g., com.example.myapp).

app_store_id.description: The App Store ID for your iOS app.

name: firebase_create_android_sha
description: Use this to add the specified SHA certificate hash to the specified Firebase Android App.

app_id.description: The Android app ID to add the SHA certificate to.

sha_hash.description: The SHA certificate hash to add (SHA-1 or SHA-256).

name: firebase_get_sdk_config
description: Use this to retrieve the Firebase configuration information for a Firebase App. You must specify EITHER a platform OR the Firebase App ID.

platform.description: The platform for which you want config. One of 'platform' or 'app_id' must be provided.

app_id.description: The specific app ID to fetch. One of 'platform' or 'app_id' must be provided.

name: firebase_get_security_rules
description: Use this to retrieve the security rules for a specified Firebase service. If there are multiple instances of that service in the product, the rules for the default instance are returned.

type.description: The service to get rules for.

name: firebase_validate_security_rules
description: Use this to validate Firebase security rules for Firestore, Realtime Database, or Storage.

type.description: The service to validate rules for.

rules.description: The security rules content to validate.

name: firebase_init
description: Use this to initialize selected Firebase services in the workspace (Cloud Firestore database, Firebase Data Connect, Firebase Realtime Database, Firebase AI Logic). All services are optional; specify only the products you want to set up.

database.description: Provide this object to initialize Firebase Realtime Database.

rules_filename.description: The file to use for Realtime Database Security Rules.

rules.description: The security rules to use for Realtime Database Security Rules.

firestore.description: Provide this object to initialize Cloud Firestore.

database_id.description: The database ID to use for Firestore.

location_id.description: The GCP region ID to set up the Firestore database.

rules_filename.description: The file to use for Firestore Security Rules.

name: firestore_query_collection
description: Use this to retrieve one or more Firestore documents from a collection in a database in the current project by a collection with a full document path.

database.description: Database id to use. Defaults to `(default)` if unspecified.

filters.description: the multiple filters to use in querying against the existing collection.

compare_value.description: One and only one value may be specified per filters object.

string_value.description: The string value to compare against.

boolean_value.description: The boolean value to compare against.

string_array_value.description: The string array value to compare against.

integer_value.description: The integer value to compare against.

double_value.description: The double value to compare against.

order.description: Specifies the field and direction to order the results.

limit.description: The maximum amount of records to return. Default is 10.

use_emulator.description: Target the Firestore emulator if true.

name: auth_get_users
description: Use this to retrieve one or more Firebase Auth users based on a list of UIDs or a list of emails.

uids.description: A list of user UIDs to retrieve.

emails.description: A list of user emails to retrieve.

phone_numbers.description: A list of user phone numbers to retrieve.

limit.description: The numbers of users to return. 500 is the upper limit. Defaults to 100.

name: auth_update_user
description: Use this to disable, enable, or set a custom claim on a specific user's account.

uid.description: the UID of the user to update

disabled.description: true disables the user, false enables the user

claim.description: Custom claim to set on the user.

key.description: the name (key) of the claim to update, e.g. 'admin'

value.description: Set the value of the custom claim to the specified simple scalar value.

json_value.description: Set the claim to a complex JSON value by providing stringified JSON.

name: auth_set_sms_region_policy
description: Use this to set an SMS region policy for Firebase Authentication to restrict the regions which can receive text messages based on an ALLOW or DENY list of country codes.

policy_type.description: with an ALLOW policy, only the specified country codes can use SMS auth. with a DENY policy, all countries can use SMS auth except the ones specified

country_codes.description: the country codes to allow or deny based on ISO 3166

name: functions_list_functions
description: List all deployed functions in your Firebase project.

name: functions_get_logs
description: Use this to retrieve a page of Cloud Functions log entries using Google Cloud Logging advanced filters.

function_names.description: Optional list of deployed Cloud Function IDs to filter logs (e.g. ['fnA','fnB']).

page_size.description: Maximum number of log entries to return.

page_token.description: Opaque page token returned from a previous call to continue pagination.

min_severity.description: Filters results to entries at or above the provided severity level.

start_time.description: RFC3339 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). Only entries with timestamp greater than or equal to this are returned.

end_time.description: RFC3339 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). Only entries with timestamp less than or equal to this are returned.

filter.description: Additional Google Cloud Logging advanced filter text that will be AND'ed with the generated filter.

name: messaging_send_message
description: Use this to send a message to a Firebase Cloud Messaging registration token or topic. ONLY ONE of `registration_token` or `topic` may be supplied in a specific call.

registration_token.description: A specific device registration token for delivery. Supply either this or topic.

topic.description: A topic name for delivery. Supply either this or registration_token.

title.description: The title of the push notification message.

body.description: The body of the push notification message.

image.description: The URL of an image that will be displayed with the notification.

name: realtimedatabase_get_data
description: Use this to retrieve data from the specified location in a Firebase Realtime Database.

databaseUrl.description: connect to the database at url. If omitted, use default database instance <project>-default-rtdb.firebasedatabase.app. Can point to emulator URL.

path.description: The path to the data to read. (ex: /my/cool/path)

name: realtimedatabase_set_data
description: Use this to write data to the specified location in a Firebase Realtime Database.

databaseUrl.description: connect to the database at url. If omitted, use default database instance. Can point to emulator URL.

path.description: The path to the data to write. (ex: /my/cool/path)

name: storage_get_object_download_url
description: Use this to retrieve the download URL for an object in a Cloud Storage for Firebase bucket.

bucket.description: The bucket name in Firebase Storage. If not provided, defaults to the project's default bucket.

object_path.description: The path to the object in Firebase storage without the bucket name attached

use_emulator.description: Target the Storage emulator if true.

name: remoteconfig_get_template
description: Use this to retrieve the specified Firebase Remote Config template from the currently active Firebase Project.

version_number.description: The version number of the template to retrieve. If not provided, retrieves the active template.

name: remoteconfig_update_template
description: Use this to publish a new remote config template or roll back to a specific version for the project.

template.description: The Remote Config template object to publish.

version_number.description: The version number to roll back to.

force.description: If true, the publish will bypass ETag validation and overwrite the current template.

name: apphosting_list_backends
description: Use this to retrieve a list of App Hosting backends in the current project. An empty list means that there are no backends.

location.description: Limit the listed backends to this region. By default, it will list all backends across all regions.

name: apphosting_fetch_logs
description: Use this to fetch the most recent logs for a specified App Hosting backend. If buildLogs is specified, the logs from the build process for the latest build are returned.

buildLogs.description: If specified, the logs for the most recent build will be returned instead of the logs for the service.

backendId.description: The ID of the backend for which to fetch logs.

location.description: The specific region for the backend.

name: apptesting_run_test
description: Run a remote test using Firebase App Distribution and Firebase Test Lab.

testDevices.description: List of devices to run the test on.

goal.description: A goal to be accomplished during the test.

hint.description: Hint text containing suggestions to help the agent accomplish the goal.

finalScreenAssertion.description: A description of what the screen should look like at the end of the step.

name: apptesting_check_status
description: Check the status of an apptesting release test and/or get available devices that can be used for automated tests.

release_test_name.description: The name of the release test returned by the run_test tool. If set, the tool will fetch the release test.

getAvailableDevices.description: If set to true, the tool will get the available devices that can be used for automated tests using the app testing agent.

name: crashlytics_get_issue
description: Gets data for a Crashlytics issue, which can be used as a starting point for debugging.

name: crashlytics_update_issue
description: Use this to update the state of a Crashlytics issue.

state.description: The new state for the issue.

name: dataconnect_list_services
description: Use this to list existing local and backend Firebase Data Connect services.

name: dataconnect_execute
description: Use this to execute a GraphQL operation against a Data Connect service or its emulator.

operationName.description: The name of the GraphQL operation to execute.

use_emulator.description: Target the Data Connect emulator if true.

name: dataconnect_compile
description: Use this to compile a Data Connect schema or connector.
